== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942年6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

[[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11年6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10年-昭和11年』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994年）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942年6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

revision.text:
[[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10年-昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994年）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942年6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

revision.text:
[[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

Miyaji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.

He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].

Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.

During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.

;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>

=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}

revision.text:
[[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''
{{Normdaten}}

== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}

revision.text:
[[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

revision.text:
[[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

[[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.

[[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Naokazu Miyaji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Naokazu Miyaji]] [[ja:宮地直一]] [[en:Naokazu Miyaji]] [[sn:aokazu Miyaji]]

{{Infobox 学者 | name = 宮地 直一 | image = Naokazu Miyaji.jpg | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1886|1|24}} | birth_place ={{JPN}}[[高知県]][[高知市]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1949|5|16|1886|1|24}} | death_place = | othername = |main_interests = [[日本史]](日本古代史・神道史) |alma_mater = [[東京大学|東京帝国大学文科大学]] |workplaces = [[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]神社局、[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]] |degree = <!--学位--> | URL = | yearsactive = | spouse = | children = }} '''宮地 直一'''（みやじ なおかず、[[1886年]]（明治19年）[[1月24日]]<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> - [[1949年]]（昭和24年）[[5月16日]]<ref name="kotobank1"/>）は、[[日本]]の[[内務省 (日本)|内務]][[官僚]]、[[神道学者]]。内務省神社局考証課長、[[東京帝国大学]]教授。近代[[実証]][[神道史|神道史学]]の確立者。
== 経歴 == ;出生から修学期 1886年、[[高知県]][[高知市]]で生まれた<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[國學院大學]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>。[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]]文科大学史学科で学び、1908年に卒業<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 『官報』第7514号、明治41年7月14日、p.378]</ref>。
;内務省勤務・東京帝国大学勤務時代(戦前) [[1909年]]、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]入省<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。神社局考証官<ref name="kokugakuin"/>、同考証課長<ref name="kokugakuin"/>などを務め、戦前の日本における神社・神道行政に大きな役割を果たした<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。内務省に勤務する一方で、大学でも教鞭を取った。1918年より、東京帝国大学文科大学講師として神祇史を担当<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。1922年からは國學院大學教授として同じく神祇史を担当した<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。
1938年、内務省を退官して東京帝国大学教授に就任。神道研究室主任教授となり、神道講座を担当した<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=国立公文書館|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>。
;太平洋戦争後。 戦後は[[神社]]存続のため[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|GHQ]]との折衝をおこなった<ref name="kotobank1"/>ほか、[[神社本庁]]の創設にも関わり<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>、同顧問を務めた<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。1946年、東京帝国大学を定年退官<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。
1949年に死去。墓所は[[多磨霊園]]にある。
== 栄典 == ;位階 * 1941年（昭和16年） - [[正四位]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;勲章 * 1940年（昭和15年） - [[瑞宝章|勲三等瑞宝章]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;外国勲章佩用允許 * [[1941年]]（昭和16年）[[12月9日]] ** [[満州国|満州帝国]]：勲三位桂国章<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942年6月20日。</ref> ** [[満州国|満州帝国]]：[[記念章#建国神廟創建紀念章|建国神廟創建記念章]]<ref name="#1"/>
==家族・親族== *父：宮地直親は高知県士族。 *弟：宮地數千木は[[アララギ派]]の歌人。[[スミレ]]研究者<ref>[https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 人事興信録データベース]</ref>。
== 研究内容・業績 == 実証史学に基づく近代神道史学の先駆者として知られる<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。一方で、東京帝国大学在学中に師事した[[萩野由之]]の影響から、信仰的な要素も見られるとの指摘がある<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。1923年に「熊野三山ヲ中心トシタル神社ノ史的変遷」により、文部大臣から[[文学博士]]の[[学位]]を授与される<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 『官報』第3249号、大正11年5月31日、p.798]</ref>。
内務省[[神社局]]では、社格の認定などに関係する神社考証を担当した<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。[[明治神宮]]の創建にも深く関わり、祭式や神宝を定める為の神宮・諸社の調査などで中心的な役割を担った<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。祭式・神宝制定のため各地の神社に調査に赴き、神社局考証課長在任時は、1929年（昭和4年）の[[神宮式年遷宮]]にて奉献される御装束神宝の古儀調査なども担当した<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。また、神道史の専門家として、各地で講義をおこなった<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。
[[1936年]]（昭和11年）には『[[国体の本義]]』編纂委員（神道担当）を務めた<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11年6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10年-昭和11年』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994年）</ref>。
== 著作 == ;著書 {{colbegin|2}} *『神祇史』皇典講究所國學院大學出版部 1910 *『神祇史綱要』[[明治書院]] 1919 *『神祇史の研究』[[古今書院]] 1924 *『神祇と国史』古今書院 1926 *『[[諏訪神社]]の研究』信濃教育会諏訪部会 1931-1937 *『諏訪史』第2巻 前編 信濃教育会諏訪部会 1931 *『神祇史大系』訂正版(3版) 明治書院 1932 *『祭神解』東方書院(日本宗教講座)1935 *『文神としての[[天満宮|天神]]の信仰』東京出版協会 1936 *『神社綱要』東洋図書 1938 *『神道論攷』第1巻 古今書院 1942 *『[[会津藩]]教学の根本精神』跋、[[若松市]]教育部会 1942 *『神道思潮』[[理想社]] 1943 *『神道史』(神道講座 1) 四海書房 1943 *『[[安曇族]]文化の信仰的象徴』[[穂高神社]]社務所 1949 *『穂高神社史』穂高神社社務所 1949 *『山嶽信仰と神社』神社新報社(山岳信仰叢書) 1949 *『遺稿集』(全6巻) 国民信仰研究所 1954-1963 {{colend}}
=== 共著・校訂など === {{colbegin|2}} *『[[延喜式]]神祇巻』[[藤原時平]]著、下田義照・三島吉太郎共校 盛文社 1910 *『神宮と[[式年遷宮]]』阪本広太郎共著 四海書房 1929 *『[[神皇正統記]]』[[北畠親房]]著、改造文庫 1929 *『国史神祇集』梅園惟朝著、大洋閣書房 1932 *『三国最上之祓』明治書院(神書叢刊) 1934 *『大祓詞註釈大成』[[山本信哉]]・[[河野省三]]共編 内外書籍 1935-38 *『[[中臣祓]]』明治書院(神書叢刊) 1938 *『[[三十番神]]絵像説』明治書院(神書叢刊) 1938 *『本朝神社考』[[林羅山|林道春]]著、改造文庫 1942 *『校註[[霊能]]真柱』[[平田篤胤]]著、明世堂書店 1944 *『中臣祓講義』[[竹内敬持|竹内式部]]著、[[岩波文庫]] 1944 {{colend}}
;記念論集 *『神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集』國學院大學神道史学会編 [[叢文社]] 1980
== 参考 == *日本人名大辞典
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

revision.text:
{{Infobox 学者 | name = 宮地 直一 | image = Naokazu Miyaji.jpg | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1886|1|24}} | birth_place ={{JPN}}[[高知県]][[高知市]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1949|5|16|1886|1|24}} | death_place = | othername = |main_interests = [[日本史]](日本古代史・神道史) |alma_mater = [[東京大学|東京帝国大学文科大学]] |workplaces = [[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]神社局、[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]] |degree = <!--学位--> | URL = | yearsactive = | spouse = | children = }} '''宮地 直一'''（みやじ なおかず、[[1886年]]（明治19年）[[1月24日]]<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> - [[1949年]]（昭和24年）[[5月16日]]<ref name="kotobank1"/>）は、[[日本]]の[[内務省 (日本)|内務]][[官僚]]、[[神道学者]]。内務省神社局考証課長、[[東京帝国大学]]教授。近代[[実証]][[神道史|神道史学]]の確立者。
== 経歴 == ;出生から修学期 1886年、[[高知県]][[高知市]]で生まれた<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[國學院大學]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>。[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]]文科大学史学科で学び、1908年に卒業<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 『官報』第7514号、明治41年7月14日、p.378]</ref>。
;内務省勤務・東京帝国大学勤務時代(戦前) [[1909年]]、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]入省<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。神社局考証官<ref name="kokugakuin"/>、同考証課長<ref name="kokugakuin"/>などを務め、戦前の日本における神社・神道行政に大きな役割を果たした<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。内務省に勤務する一方で、大学でも教鞭を取った。1918年より、東京帝国大学文科大学講師として神祇史を担当<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。1922年からは國學院大學教授として同じく神祇史を担当した<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。
1938年、内務省を退官して東京帝国大学教授に就任。神道研究室主任教授となり、神道講座を担当した<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=国立公文書館|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>。
;太平洋戦争後。 戦後は[[神社]]存続のため[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|GHQ]]との折衝をおこなった<ref name="kotobank1"/>ほか、[[神社本庁]]の創設にも関わり<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>、同顧問を務めた<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。1946年、東京帝国大学を定年退官<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。
1949年に死去。墓所は[[多磨霊園]]にある。
== 栄典 == ;位階 * 1941年（昭和16年） - [[正四位]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;勲章 * 1940年（昭和15年） - [[瑞宝章|勲三等瑞宝章]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;外国勲章佩用允許 * [[1941年]]（昭和16年）[[12月9日]] ** [[満州国|満州帝国]]：勲三位桂国章<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942年6月20日。</ref> ** [[満州国|満州帝国]]：[[記念章#建国神廟創建紀念章|建国神廟創建記念章]]<ref name="#1"/>
==家族・親族== *父：宮地直親は高知県士族。 *弟：宮地數千木は[[アララギ派]]の歌人。[[スミレ]]研究者<ref>[https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 人事興信録データベース]</ref>。
== 研究内容・業績 == 実証史学に基づく近代神道史学の先駆者として知られる<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。一方で、東京帝国大学在学中に師事した[[萩野由之]]の影響から、信仰的な要素も見られるとの指摘がある<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。1923年に「熊野三山ヲ中心トシタル神社ノ史的変遷」により、文部大臣から[[文学博士]]の[[学位]]を授与される<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 『官報』第3249号、大正11年5月31日、p.798]</ref>。
内務省[[神社局]]では、社格の認定などに関係する神社考証を担当した<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。[[明治神宮]]の創建にも深く関わり、祭式や神宝を定める為の神宮・諸社の調査などで中心的な役割を担った<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。祭式・神宝制定のため各地の神社に調査に赴き、神社局考証課長在任時は、1929年（昭和4年）の[[神宮式年遷宮]]にて奉献される御装束神宝の古儀調査なども担当した<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。また、神道史の専門家として、各地で講義をおこなった<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。
[[1936年]]（昭和11年）には『[[国体の本義]]』編纂委員（神道担当）を務めた<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11年6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10年-昭和11年』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994年）</ref>。
== 著作 == ;著書 {{colbegin|2}} *『神祇史』皇典講究所國學院大學出版部 1910 *『神祇史綱要』[[明治書院]] 1919 *『神祇史の研究』[[古今書院]] 1924 *『神祇と国史』古今書院 1926 *『[[諏訪神社]]の研究』信濃教育会諏訪部会 1931-1937 *『諏訪史』第2巻 前編 信濃教育会諏訪部会 1931 *『神祇史大系』訂正版(3版) 明治書院 1932 *『祭神解』東方書院(日本宗教講座)1935 *『文神としての[[天満宮|天神]]の信仰』東京出版協会 1936 *『神社綱要』東洋図書 1938 *『神道論攷』第1巻 古今書院 1942 *『[[会津藩]]教学の根本精神』跋、[[若松市]]教育部会 1942 *『神道思潮』[[理想社]] 1943 *『神道史』(神道講座 1) 四海書房 1943 *『[[安曇族]]文化の信仰的象徴』[[穂高神社]]社務所 1949 *『穂高神社史』穂高神社社務所 1949 *『山嶽信仰と神社』神社新報社(山岳信仰叢書) 1949 *『遺稿集』(全6巻) 国民信仰研究所 1954-1963 {{colend}}
=== 共著・校訂など === {{colbegin|2}} *『[[延喜式]]神祇巻』[[藤原時平]]著、下田義照・三島吉太郎共校 盛文社 1910 *『神宮と[[式年遷宮]]』阪本広太郎共著 四海書房 1929 *『[[神皇正統記]]』[[北畠親房]]著、改造文庫 1929 *『国史神祇集』梅園惟朝著、大洋閣書房 1932 *『三国最上之祓』明治書院(神書叢刊) 1934 *『大祓詞註釈大成』[[山本信哉]]・[[河野省三]]共編 内外書籍 1935-38 *『[[中臣祓]]』明治書院(神書叢刊) 1938 *『[[三十番神]]絵像説』明治書院(神書叢刊) 1938 *『本朝神社考』[[林羅山|林道春]]著、改造文庫 1942 *『校註[[霊能]]真柱』[[平田篤胤]]著、明世堂書店 1944 *『中臣祓講義』[[竹内敬持|竹内式部]]著、[[岩波文庫]] 1944 {{colend}}
;記念論集 *『神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集』國學院大學神道史学会編 [[叢文社]] 1980
== 参考 == *日本人名大辞典

{{Infobox 学者 | name = 宮地 直一 | image = Naokazu Miyaji.jpg | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1886|1|24}} | birth_place ={{JPN}}[[高知県]][[高知市]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1949|5|16|1886|1|24}} | death_place = | othername = |main_interests = [[日本史]](日本古代史・神道史) |alma_mater = [[東京大学|東京帝国大学文科大学]] |workplaces = [[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]神社局、[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]] |degree = <!--学位--> | URL = | yearsactive = | spouse = | children = }} '''宮地 直一'''（みやじ なおかず、[[1886年]]（明治19年）[[1月24日]]<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> - [[1949年]]（昭和24年）[[5月16日]]<ref name="kotobank1"/>）は、[[日本]]の[[内務省 (日本)|内務]][[官僚]]、[[神道学者]]。内務省神社局考証課長、[[東京帝国大学]]教授。近代[[実証]][[神道史|神道史学]]の確立者。
== 経歴 == ;出生から修学期 1886年、[[高知県]][[高知市]]で生まれた<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[國學院大學]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>。[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]]文科大学史学科で学び、1908年に卒業<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 『官報』第7514号、明治41年7月14日、p.378]</ref>。
;内務省勤務・東京帝国大学勤務時代(戦前) [[1909年]]、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]入省<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。神社局考証官<ref name="kokugakuin"/>、同考証課長<ref name="kokugakuin"/>などを務め、戦前の日本における神社・神道行政に大きな役割を果たした<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。内務省に勤務する一方で、大学でも教鞭を取った。1918年より、東京帝国大学文科大学講師として神祇史を担当<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。1922年からは國學院大學教授として同じく神祇史を担当した<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。
1938年、内務省を退官して東京帝国大学教授に就任。神道研究室主任教授となり、神道講座を担当した<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=国立公文書館|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>。
;太平洋戦争後。 戦後は[[神社]]存続のため[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|GHQ]]との折衝をおこなった<ref name="kotobank1"/>ほか、[[神社本庁]]の創設にも関わり<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>、同顧問を務めた<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。1946年、東京帝国大学を定年退官<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。
1949年に死去。墓所は[[多磨霊園]]にある。
== 栄典 == ;位階 * 1941年（昭和16年） - [[正四位]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;勲章 * 1940年（昭和15年） - [[瑞宝章|勲三等瑞宝章]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;外国勲章佩用允許 * [[1941年]]（昭和16年）[[12月9日]] ** [[満州国|満州帝国]]：勲三位桂国章<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942年6月20日。</ref> ** [[満州国|満州帝国]]：[[記念章#建国神廟創建紀念章|建国神廟創建記念章]]<ref name="#1"/>
==家族・親族== *父：宮地直親は高知県士族。 *弟：宮地數千木は[[アララギ派]]の歌人。[[スミレ]]研究者<ref>[https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 人事興信録データベース]</ref>。
== 研究内容・業績 == 実証史学に基づく近代神道史学の先駆者として知られる<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。一方で、東京帝国大学在学中に師事した[[萩野由之]]の影響から、信仰的な要素も見られるとの指摘がある<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。1923年に「熊野三山ヲ中心トシタル神社ノ史的変遷」により、文部大臣から[[文学博士]]の[[学位]]を授与される<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 『官報』第3249号、大正11年5月31日、p.798]</ref>。
内務省[[神社局]]では、社格の認定などに関係する神社考証を担当した<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。[[明治神宮]]の創建にも深く関わり、祭式や神宝を定める為の神宮・諸社の調査などで中心的な役割を担った<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。祭式・神宝制定のため各地の神社に調査に赴き、神社局考証課長在任時は、1929年（昭和4年）の[[神宮式年遷宮]]にて奉献される御装束神宝の古儀調査なども担当した<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。また、神道史の専門家として、各地で講義をおこなった<ref name="kokugakuin"/>。
[[1936年]]（昭和11年）には『[[国体の本義]]』編纂委員（神道担当）を務めた<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11年6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10年-昭和11年』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994年）</ref>。
== 著作 == ;著書 {{colbegin|2}} *『神祇史』皇典講究所國學院大學出版部 1910 *『神祇史綱要』[[明治書院]] 1919 *『神祇史の研究』[[古今書院]] 1924 *『神祇と国史』古今書院 1926 *『[[諏訪神社]]の研究』信濃教育会諏訪部会 1931-1937 *『諏訪史』第2巻 前編 信濃教育会諏訪部会 1931 *『神祇史大系』訂正版(3版) 明治書院 1932 *『祭神解』東方書院(日本宗教講座)1935 *『文神としての[[天満宮|天神]]の信仰』東京出版協会 1936 *『神社綱要』東洋図書 1938 *『神道論攷』第1巻 古今書院 1942 *『[[会津藩]]教学の根本精神』跋、[[若松市]]教育部会 1942 *『神道思潮』[[理想社]] 1943 *『神道史』(神道講座 1) 四海書房 1943 *『[[安曇族]]文化の信仰的象徴』[[穂高神社]]社務所 1949 *『穂高神社史』穂高神社社務所 1949 *『山嶽信仰と神社』神社新報社(山岳信仰叢書) 1949 *『遺稿集』(全6巻) 国民信仰研究所 1954-1963 {{colend}}
=== 共著・校訂など === {{colbegin|2}} *『[[延喜式]]神祇巻』[[藤原時平]]著、下田義照・三島吉太郎共校 盛文社 1910 *『神宮と[[式年遷宮]]』阪本広太郎共著 四海書房 1929 *『[[神皇正統記]]』[[北畠親房]]著、改造文庫 1929 *『国史神祇集』梅園惟朝著、大洋閣書房 1932 *『三国最上之祓』明治書院(神書叢刊) 1934 *『大祓詞註釈大成』[[山本信哉]]・[[河野省三]]共編 内外書籍 1935-38 *『[[中臣祓]]』明治書院(神書叢刊) 1938 *『[[三十番神]]絵像説』明治書院(神書叢刊) 1938 *『本朝神社考』[[林羅山|林道春]]著、改造文庫 1942 *『校註[[霊能]]真柱』[[平田篤胤]]著、明世堂書店 1944 *『中臣祓講義』[[竹内敬持|竹内式部]]著、[[岩波文庫]] 1944 {{colend}}
;記念論集 *『神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集』國學院大學神道史学会編 [[叢文社]] 1980
== 参考 == *日本人名大辞典
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}

revision.text:
[[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[Kōchi City]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Miyaji graduated from the [[University of Tokyo]] Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = [[Kokugakuin University]] | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at [[Kokugakuin University]] in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine Rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Jingu|Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the [[1929]] [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''
==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}

Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. His final resting place is in [[Tama Cemetery]].
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: [[Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** [[Manchukuo]]: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** [[Manchukuo]]: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
