revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮地直一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮地直一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮地直一]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|birth_date=1886; {{birth date|1886|1|24}}|death_date=1949; {{death date|1949|5|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|teacher|WD=Q37226}}|awards={{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class|WD=Q56561400}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11453535]] Wikidata name: Naokazu Miyachi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[:en:Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[:en:Araragi school|Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[:en:Kōchi City|Kōchi City]], {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Miyaji graduated from the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo}} Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[:en:Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}} | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}} in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in {{ill|Tama Cemetery|en|Tama Cemetery|WD=Q982473|lt=Tama Cemetery}}.
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine Rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Jingu|Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}} [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: {{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure|en|Order of the Sacred Treasure|WD=Q1066052|lt=Order of the Sacred Treasure}}, Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo}}: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo}}: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine Rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Jingu|Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}} [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: {{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure|en|Order of the Sacred Treasure|WD=Q1066052|lt=Order of the Sacred Treasure}}, Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo}}: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo}}: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''
==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮地直一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮地直一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮地直一]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|birth_date=1886; {{birth date|1886|1|24}}|death_date=1949; {{death date|1949|5|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|teacher|WD=Q37226}}|awards={{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class|WD=Q56561400}}}} [[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[:en:Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[:en:Araragi school|Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[:en:Kōchi City|Kōchi City]], {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Miyaji graduated from the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo}} Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[:en:Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}} | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}} in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in {{ill|Tama Cemetery|en|Tama Cemetery|WD=Q982473|lt=Tama Cemetery}}.
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine Rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Jingu|Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}} [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: {{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure|en|Order of the Sacred Treasure|WD=Q1066052|lt=Order of the Sacred Treasure}}, Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo}}: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo}}: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''

He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}} in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in {{ill|Tama Cemetery|en|Tama Cemetery|WD=Q982473|lt=Tama Cemetery|1=Tama Cemetery}}.
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine Rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Jingu|Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}} [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: {{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure|en|Order of the Sacred Treasure|WD=Q1066052|lt=Order of the Sacred Treasure|1=Order of the Sacred Treasure}}, Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo|1=Manchukuo}}: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo|1=Manchukuo}}: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''
==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}

He entered the [[:en:Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}} | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮地直一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮地直一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮地直一]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|birth_date=1886; {{birth date|1886|1|24}}|death_date=1949; {{death date|1949|5|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|teacher|WD=Q37226|1=Teacher}}|awards={{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class|WD=Q56561400|1=Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class}}}} [[File:Naokazu Miyaji.jpg|thumb|Naokazu Miyaji]] '''Naokazu Miyaji''' (宮地直一, January 24, 1886<ref name="kotobank1">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref> – May 16, 1949<ref name="kotobank1"/>) was a Japanese [[:en:Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] official and a [[Shinto]] scholar. He is known as a pioneer of modern [[empirical historical Shinto studies]]. His younger brother, [https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-21853 Kazuchigi Miyaji], was a researcher of violets and a poet of the [[:en:Araragi school|Araragi school]].
== Early Life and Career == Born in [[:en:Kōchi City|Kōchi City]], {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Miyaji graduated from the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo|1=University of Tokyo}} Faculty of Letters, Department of History, in 1908<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2950861/11 官報, July 14, 1908, Issue 7514, p.378]</ref>. He entered the [[:en:Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]] in 1909<ref name="kokugakuin">{{Cite web|和書| url = http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/frontier/projects/miyaji.html | title = 國學院大學学術フロンティア／宮地直一博士資料の研究 | publisher = {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}} | date = | accessdate = 2017-01-09 }}</ref>. He held positions such as Shinto Shrine Bureau Examiner and Chief of the Examination Division, playing a significant role in Japan’s prewar shrine and Shinto administration<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
Miyaji also taught at universities during his tenure. He served as a lecturer in Shinto history at the University of Tokyo in 1918<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, became a professor at {{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}} in 1922<ref name="kokugakuin"/>, and was appointed head professor of the Shinto Studies Department at the University of Tokyo in 1938<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/img/3231883|title=「考証官宮地直一任官ノ件」『任免裁可書・昭和十三年・任免巻二十二』|date=1938-03-11|website=|publisher=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2023-03-07}}</ref>. He retired from the Home Ministry the same year and from the University of Tokyo in 1946<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
After World War II, Miyaji negotiated with the [[Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] to ensure the continuation of shrines<ref name="kotobank1"/>. He also contributed to the establishment of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] and served as its advisor<ref name="kotobank2">{{Cite kotobank|宮地直一(みやじなおかず)|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2017-01-09}}</ref>. His final resting place is in {{ill|Tama Cemetery|en|Tama Cemetery|WD=Q982473|lt=Tama Cemetery|1=Tama Cemetery}}.
== Contributions == Miyaji was a pioneer in applying empirical historiography to Shinto studies<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. Influenced by his teacher [[Yoshiyuki Hagino]] during his time at the University of Tokyo, his work also exhibited spiritual elements<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. In 1923, he was awarded a Doctorate of Letters for his work on the historical transitions of [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]]<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2955372/10 官報, May 31, 1923, Issue 3249, p.798]</ref>.
As a Home Ministry official, Miyaji was responsible for shrine examinations related to shrine Rankings<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. He played a central role in the creation of the [[Meiji Jingu|Meiji Shrine]] and in the establishment of its rituals and sacred treasures<ref name="kokugakuin"/>. During his tenure, he conducted numerous investigations into traditional rites and ancient treasures for the {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}} [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] of Ise Shrine<ref name="kokugakuin"/>.
In 1936, he served on the editorial committee for ''[[The Cardinal Principles of the National Polity]]'' (''Kokutai no Hongi'') as the Shinto representative<ref>「国体の本義」編纂委員決まる『大阪毎日新聞』昭和11th year 6月2日（『昭和ニュース事典第5巻 昭和10th year -昭和11th year 』本編p712 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994th year ）</ref>.
== Honors == ;Rank * 1941: [[Senior Fourth Rank]]<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Decorations * 1940: {{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure|en|Order of the Sacred Treasure|WD=Q1066052|lt=Order of the Sacred Treasure|1=Order of the Sacred Treasure}}, Third Class<ref name="kokugakuin"/>
;Foreign Decorations * 1941: ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo|1=Manchukuo}}: Third Class [[Order of the Pillars of State]]<ref name="#1">『官報』第4632号 付録「辞令二」1942nd year 6月20日。</ref> ** {{ill|Manchukuo|en|Manchukuo|WD=Q30623|lt=Manchukuo|1=Manchukuo}}: [[Commemorative Medal of the National Shrine Establishment]]<ref name="#1"/>
== Selected Works == {{colbegin|2}} *''Shinji-shi'' (神祇史), Kōten Kōkyūjo Kokugakuin Daigaku, 1910 *''Shinji-shi Kōyō'' (神祇史綱要), Meiji Shoin, 1919 *''Research on the History of Shinto'' (神祇史の研究), Kokin Shoin, 1924 *''Shinto and National History'' (神祇と国史), Kokin Shoin, 1926 *''Research on Suwa Shrine'' (諏訪神社の研究), Shinano Education Society, 1931-37 *''Suwa History, Volume 2'' (諏訪史), Shinano Education Society, 1931 *''Shinji-shi Taikei'' (神祇史大系), Revised Edition, Meiji Shoin, 1932 *''Explanations of Kami Worship'' (祭神解), Tōhō Shoin, 1935 *''Faith in Tenjin as a Cultural God'' (文神としての天神の信仰), Tokyo Publishing Association, 1936 *''Outline of Shinto'' (神社綱要), Tōyō Tosho, 1938 *''Shinto Thought'' (神道思潮), Riso-sha, 1943 *''Mountains and Shrines'' (山嶽信仰と神社), Shrine Shimbun Publishing, 1949 {{colend}}
=== Editorial and Collaborative Works === {{colbegin|2}} *''Engishiki'' (延喜式), co-edited with Yoshiteru Shimoda, Sebunsha, 1910 *''Jingu and Shikinen Sengu'' (神宮と式年遷宮), co-authored with Kōtarō Sakamoto, Shikai Shobō, 1929 *''Kōnin Seiji'' (皇任正統記), Kaizō Bunko, 1929 *''Collected Writings on Kami Worship'' (神祇集), Taiyōkaku Shoin, 1932 *''Commentary on Ōharai'' (大祓詞註釈大成), co-edited with Yamamoto Nobuchika and Kōno Seizō, Uchigai Shoten, 1935-38 *''Chūnori Harai'' (中臣祓), Meiji Shoin, 1938 *''The Divine Essence of the Nation'' (霊能真柱), edited by Atsutane Hirata, Myoseido Shoten, 1944 {{colend}}
== Commemorative Publications == *''Research on Shinto History: Essays in Memory of Dr. Naokazu Miyaji'' (神道史の研究 宮地直一博士三十年祭記念論文集), Kokugakuin University Shinto Historical Society, Sōbunsha, 1980.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Japanese Biographical Dictionary''
==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|宮地直一|version=104509199|comment=Imported full ja history}}
